title: coluteastrum excisum
intro: Coluteastrum excisum, also known as the cut-leaved toothwort or cut-leaved coltsfoot, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The health benefits associated with Coluteastrum excisum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in modern medicine, and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
